You have a clear green light to finalize the Brookfield FP-2026-05 management accounts package.\\n\\nBest,\\nMargaret\", \"cc\": [\"andrea.phil@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"parent_id\": \"email_scen_024_monthly_close_0009\", \"attachments\": {}, \"timestamp\": \"2026-06-05T15:48:00Z\", \"is_read\": true}, {\"email_id\": \"email_scen_010_orphan_exception_0009\", \"folder\": \"INBOX\", \"sender\": \"hannah.grant@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"recipients\": [\"george.mcadam@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"subject\": \"Re: Disposition recommendation for exc_1ddfc978ce5a4d on BL-75810CD0FEE4\", \"content\": \"Hi George,\\n\\nApproved to accept timing for FP-2026-05 on exc_1ddfc978ce5a4d tied to BL-75810CD0FEE4 and account 119000. Please leave the underlying exception referenced as-is for now rather than changing the disposition trail until we see whether the June rollforward lands.\\n\\nRevisit this at FP-2026-06 BD3. Please ask Anaya to flag in Slack whether the June engagement-hours rollforward is delivered or still missing so the coordination thread stays live and we can decide then whether the item clears or needs escalation.\\n\\nThanks,\\nHannah\", \"cc\": [], \"parent_id\": \"email_scen_010_orphan_exception_0008\", \"attachments\": {}, \"timestamp\": \"2026-06-02T19:12:00Z\", \"is_read\": false}, {\"email_id\": \"email_scen_036_ap_escalation_0010\", \"folder\": \"INBOX\", \"sender\": \"matthew.li@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"recipients\": [\"daniel.jones@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"subject\": \"Re: northstar_legal CrownPeak close-out summary and sign-off request\", \"content\": \"Hi Daniel,\\n\\nI approve the void narrative for apinv_e42396e9bdd64294. On the facts here, VEN-030-736427 is a stale pre-bill tied to the 2026-02-28 cancellation, and we should close it as a void on our side.\\n\\nThe CrownPeak credit-memo backfill is non-negotiable for bookkeeping close, so please keep that on the follow-through list until Brenda has it in hand. More importantly, Anaya's observation is the real finding: if a cancellation event can occur without triggering an AP-side cross-check against pending items, that is a control gap and we should treat it that way. Glad to hear Priya is already drafting the policy framing.\\n\\nMonday 2026-06-08 at 14:00 works for me. Please send the invite to me, you, Owen, Brenda, Anaya, and Priya so we can align on both the CrownPeak cleanup and whether that cancellation-event cross-check needs to become a standing control.\\n\\nBest,\\nMatthew\", \"cc\": [], \"parent_id\": \"email_scen_036_ap_escalation_0009\", \"attachments\": {}, \"timestamp\": \"2026-05-31T14:19:00Z\", \"is_read\": true}, {\"email_id\": \"email_scen_009_orphan_exception_0007\", \"folder\": \"INBOX\", \"sender\": \"daniel.jones@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"recipients\": [\"blue.evans@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"subject\": \"Re: Disposition approval request: exc_aade06f6129e43 for FP-2026-05 cash-payroll feed drop\", \"content\": \"Hi Blue,\\n\\nApproved. Accept-timing for FP-2026-05 is fine based on the 29 dropped rows, Ryan's note, George's precedent, and Hannah's downstream review.\\n\\nIf the June 2 retry does not clear, re-verify at FP-2026-06 BD3 and confirm whether anything changed in the feed behavior before we revisit disposition. Until then, leave exc_aade06f6129e43 in logged state.\\n\\nThanks,\\nDaniel\", \"cc\": [], \"parent_id\": null, \"attachments\": {}, \"timestamp\": \"2026-05-30T19:51:00Z\", \"is_read\": false}, {\"email_id\": \"email_scen_022_orphan_exception_0008\", \"folder\": \"INBOX\", \"sender\": \"andrea.phil@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"recipients\": [\"emily.adekole@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"subject\": \"Re: Approval request: exc_4d5d3582698946 duplicate-entry issue on 131000 due May 30\", \"content\": \"Hi Emily,\\n\\nPartner-sight noted on the $6,068.91 amount and Daniel's proposed accounting path.\\n\\nWhat stands out to me is not just the duplicate posting but the vendor-management gap behind the May 22 manual re-key. If a renewal can be manually re-entered without clear confirmation of the active subscription footprint, that points to a broader controls concern around how vendor changes are being validated before they hit the books.\\n\\nNo change from my side on the accounting mechanics Daniel approved. Please keep the exception in investigating until the correction is prepared and the side-workstream is closed through the IT/CIO check.\\n\\nBest,\\nAndrea\", \"cc\": [\"daniel.jones@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"jones.harrison@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"ben.arinzo@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"parent_id\": \"email_scen_022_orphan_exception_0006\", \"attachments\": {}, \"timestamp\": \"2026-05-30T12:27:00Z\", \"is_read\": false}, {\"email_id\": \"email_scen_022_orphan_exception_0007\", \"folder\": \"INBOX\", \"sender\": \"daniel.jones@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"recipients\": [\"emily.adekole@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"subject\": \"Re: Approval request: exc_4d5d3582698946 duplicate-entry issue on 131000 due May 30\", \"content\": \"Hi Emily,\\n\\nApproved on the disposition path.\\n\\nUse FP-2026-05 and book the correction as DR Accounts Payable - External Vendors 210000 and CR Prepaid Software Subscriptions 131000 for $6,068.91. This should reverse the May 22 manual re-key rather than touch the May 15 auto-renewal entry.\\n\\nI will handle the IT/CIO follow-up on seat count and make sure we confirm there is only one active subscription population going forward. Keep the exception in investigating until the entry is actually prepared and we have that vendor-side confirmation in hand.\\n\\nThanks,\\nDaniel\", \"cc\": [\"andrea.phil@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"jones.harrison@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"ben.arinzo@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"parent_id\": \"email_scen_022_orphan_exception_0006\", \"attachments\": {}, \"timestamp\": \"2026-05-30T12:25:00Z\", \"is_read\": true}, {\"email_id\": \"email_scen_036_ap_escalation_0005\", \"folder\": \"INBOX\", \"sender\": \"brenda.abbas@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"recipients\": [\"owen.mercer@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"daniel.jones@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"subject\": \"Re: CrownPeak VEN-030-736427 review summary and recommended stale-void disposition\", \"content\": \"Hi Owen,\\n\\nI heard back from CrownPeak AR before close. They confirmed the 2026-02-28 mutual termination of the northstar_legal Q1 2026 IR contingency retainer is on their record, but their AR system did not auto-generate a credit memo because the pre-bill had already converted to an invoice before the cancellation was entered in their billing module.\\n\\nTheir AR lead said they will issue a manual credit memo for VEN-030-736427 within five business days. She also noted they have seen the same pre-bill/cancellation miss for other clients when the cancellation is logged after the invoice has converted, so this appears to be a trigger-logic gap on the vendor side as well, not just a filing gap in our packet.\\n\\nGiven that confirmation, I agree the Brookfield-side disposition should stay stale void with the credit-memo backfill tracked to receipt. I will follow up if the manual memo is not in hand by next week.\\n\\nThanks,\\nBrenda\", \"cc\": [], \"parent_id\": \"email_scen_036_ap_escalation_0004\", \"attachments\": {}, \"timestamp\": \"2026-05-29T21:30:00Z\", \"is_read\": true}, {\"email_id\": \"f9991525e1a14fee9a74092f90441b91\", \"folder\": \"INBOX\", \"sender\": \"julia.vance@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"recipients\": [\"maya.streamer@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"subject\": \"Re: Clarifying Monday scan items for exc_151b0bee7e374e and BL-B9133CB7D192\", \"content\": \"Hello Maya,\\n\\nCould you confirm which items from the Friday compliance deadlines summary should stay on the next-week risk scan as audit-sensitive exposures? content.text:
I now have the core picture. Let me note two important things I'm seeing and dig deeper into the support trail, the recognition figure source, the Slack thread, and the subledger feeds.

Key observations so far:
- Recon **BL-75810CD0FEE4** is in `in_progress` (not submitted), variance **$4,390.62**, Anaya's explanation already says "Subledger feed retry produced a partial-success batch; remainder pending." No attachments.
- There's an **open reviewer note from Edith Banda** (rn_564e65ce0d594f): "Foreign-currency revaluation entry referenced in the workbook - is this in scope for this period?" — George may not have seen this.
- Hannah's email confirms accept-timing for May but explicitly says **leave the exception referenced as-is** until the June rollforward lands; revisit FP-2026-06 BD3, and have Anaya flag in Slack.

Let me pull the supporting evidence and the recognition figure source.

That probably closes out the vault breadcrumb from my side, so accept-timing for FP-2026-05 looks like the likely path unless Hannah wants us to escalate further.\", \"user\": \"persona_007\", \"thread_ts\": \"1780248600.000000\", \"client_msg_id\": \"scenario_scen_010_orphan_exception_edith_reports_no_matching_doc_0\"}], \"has_more\": false}"}

She remembers the engagement-hours rollforward landing, but not a complete rollforward or source support for the residual amount.\\n\\nI also had Edith do a records-vault sweep against the May WIP feed area. She found the same partial-success rollforward Anaya referenced, but no orphaned source document or other metadata trail that would support the missing $4,390.62 detail.\\n\\nFor precedent, Jones flagged a similar pattern from FP-2026-03 where a partial batch left an unsupported residual that cleared in the next period after the feed was rerun. BROOKFIELD CPAs & ADVISORS — INTERNAL WORKING PAPER
==================================================================
MEMO: May 2026 (FP-2026-05) WIP-to-Revenue Recognition — Support Memo & Stage Schedule
Entity:            Brookfield (firm books)
Prepared by:       George McAdam, Accounts Senior
Date:              2026-06-12
Classification:    Restricted   |   Retention: AICPA_SQMS_7Y (7 years)
Related JE:        JE-brookfield-brookfield_FP-2026-05-0057 (je_53962aed96fe4b67)
Related recon:     BL-75810CD0FEE4 (Account 119000 — Work in Process – Unbilled Services)
Basis document:    Andrea Phil engagement-stage completion review (May 2026)

------------------------------------------------------------------
1. PURPOSE
------------------------------------------------------------------
Document and support the May 2026 WIP-to-revenue recognition of $147,825 across three
service lines that crossed engagement-stage milestones in May, and record the disposition
of the residual variance on the anchor WIP reconciliation. This memo is written to stand
on its own as the working-paper record behind the management package.

------------------------------------------------------------------
2. RECOGNITION AMOUNT & STAGE SCHEDULE
------------------------------------------------------------------
Andrea Phil's engagement-stage completion review identified three service lines that
crossed milestones in May 2026, totaling $147,825. Recognition by service line / GL
revenue account (the "stage schedule"):

   Service line                         GL acct   Amount recognized (CR)
   Audit & Assurance                    401000        $ 89,400.00
   Tax Preparation & Planning           402000        $ 32,250.00
   Advisory & Consulting                403000        $ 26,175.00
   --------------------------------------------------------------
   TOTAL RECOGNIZED                                   $147,825.00

   Offsetting debits per the posted JE:
   Accounts Receivable – Client Billings   110000     $ 89,425.00   (billed portion)
   Deferred Revenue – Client Retainers     240000     $ 58,400.00   (retainer release)
   --------------------------------------------------------------
   TOTAL DEBITS                                       $147,825.00

Tie-out: Andrea's review total = posted JE total = stage-schedule total = $147,825.00.
CONFIRMED across records.

------------------------------------------------------------------
3. STATUS OF THE RECOGNITION ENTRY — ALREADY POSTED  (read-changing item)
------------------------------------------------------------------
JE-brookfield-brookfield_FP-2026-05-0057 was:
   prepared by  George McAdam      2026-05-31 09:05 ET
   submitted                       2026-05-31 10:34 ET
   approved by  Margaret Sullivan  2026-05-31 11:59 ET
   posted by    Margaret Sullivan  2026-05-31 13:10 ET   STATUS: POSTED

The $147,825 is already recorded in the May ledger. No further staging is required and a
second entry must NOT be created — doing so would double-count revenue.
NOTE: this entry was reviewed/approved at partner level by Margaret Sullivan, not routed to
Daniel Jones. It is already posted, and Margaret has separately green-lit finalizing the
May management accounts package.

------------------------------------------------------------------
4. ANCHOR RECONCILIATION BL-75810CD0FEE4 — END-TO-END WALK
------------------------------------------------------------------
Account 119000 — Work in Process – Unbilled Services, FP-2026-05.
   GL balance:          $2,139,388.27
   Supporting balance:  $2,134,997.65
   Variance:            $    4,390.62  (GL > supporting; above $10 threshold)
   State:               in_progress (preparer: Anaya Wallace; no reviewer/certifier yet)
   Attachments at pull:  none (evidence added with this memo — see §8)

Variance explanation of record (Anaya Wallace):
   "Subledger feed retry produced a partial-success batch; remainder pending."

Disposition: ACCEPT TIMING for FP-2026-05, approved in writing by Hannah Grant
(email 2026-06-02, "Re: Disposition recommendation for exc_1ddfc978ce5a4d on
BL-75810CD0FEE4"). The $4,390.62 residual is PARKED as next-period cleanup per Andrea
Phil's instruction and is excluded from this recognition cycle. The underlying exception
is left referenced as-is per Hannah; to be revisited at FP-2026-06 BD3 (GL lock
2026-07-03).

------------------------------------------------------------------
5. RELATED EXCEPTION (left referenced as-is)
------------------------------------------------------------------
exc_1ddfc978ce5a4d — missing_accrual_variance, urgency high, SOX-flagged, $4,390.62 on
119000. Identified by George McAdam 2026-05-31; assigned Anaya Wallace; state: investigating
(SLA 2026-06-01, escalated). Per Hannah's instruction the exception disposition trail is NOT
changed this cycle; it remains referenced for the FP-2026-06 BD3 revisit.

------------------------------------------------------------------
6. OPEN REVIEWER NOTE  (FLAG — not previously surfaced)
------------------------------------------------------------------
rn_564e65ce0d594f — author Edith Banda, state OPEN, SLA 2026-06-02:
   "Foreign-currency revaluation entry referenced in the workbook – is this in scope for
    this period?"
This note is unresolved and will block reconciliation approval/certification until cleared.
Routed to George McAdam for a substantive answer before the recon advances. (119000 is a
domestic WIP account; whether an FX revaluation line belongs in this workbook needs a look
at the workbook itself, so it is flagged rather than assumed out of scope.)

------------------------------------------------------------------
7. SUBLEDGER RUN SUBSTRATE  (FLAG — basis discrepancy)
------------------------------------------------------------------
Brookfield Time & WIP feed (brookfield_time_and_wip_feed; source: engagement_time),
FP-2026-05 run run_e33ed2561f2c46:
   status SUCCESS | rows_in 2,083 / rows_posted 2,083 / rows_rejected 0 | no retry
   finished 2026-05-31 22:35 ET.
There was NO partial-success batch and NO retry on the WIP feed in May. The only recent
failure on this feed was FP-2026-03 (full failure, ticket INC4471157); the FP-2026-03
"partial batch" cited in the Slack triage thread was a different feed/account (133000), not
119000.

Implication: the GL side of 119000 is complete. The $4,390.62 gap sits on the SUPPORTING /
engagement-hours rollforward side, not in a dropped GL feed batch. The "feed retry / partial
batch" rationale underpinning the accept-timing call is therefore NOT corroborated by the GL
feed-run record. Accept-timing is retained per Hannah's written approval, but the FP-2026-06
BD3 revisit should test the supporting-side rollforward gap directly rather than assume a
feed re-run will deliver the missing detail.

------------------------------------------------------------------
8. EVIDENCE / SUPPORT TRAIL
------------------------------------------------------------------
- This memo + stage schedule: doc_42c851aed8fb40ab (vault, restricted, AICPA_SQMS_7Y).
- Engagement-stage backup: "Supporting documentation for WIP recognition – billable time
  logged" (doc_edaa14841cee45ee) — the engagement-hours rollforward Anaya/Edith reference.
  Edith's records-vault sweep (Slack, C005) found no orphan source document for the residual.
- Hannah Grant accept-timing approval — email 2026-06-02.
- Close-coordination triage thread — Slack #monthly-close-coordination (thread 1780248600).
- Posted recognition entry — je_53962aed96fe4b67.

------------------------------------------------------------------
9. NEXT STEPS
------------------------------------------------------------------
- FP-2026-06 BD3 (2026-07-03): revisit exc_1ddfc978ce5a4d / BL-75810CD0FEE4. Anaya to flag
  in Slack whether the June engagement-hours rollforward landed; decide clear vs escalate.
  Given the clean May feed, focus the test on the supporting-side rollforward gap.
- Clear Edith Banda's open reviewer note (rn_564e65ce0d594f) before the recon is submitted
  for approval/certification.
==================================================================

input.subject: May WIP recognition ($147,825) — status & residual parked per your note
input.content:
Hi Andrea,

Closing out the May Brookfield WIP recognition work ahead of the management package review.

Recognition status — done and tied out:
- The $147,825 from your engagement-stage completion review is recognized across the three service lines that crossed milestones in May:
    Audit & Assurance (401000)        $89,400
    Tax Preparation & Planning (402000)  $32,250
    Advisory & Consulting (403000)       $26,175
    Total                                $147,825
- It is recorded under JE-brookfield-brookfield_FP-2026-05-0057 (status: posted). The figure ties out cleanly across your review, the posted entry, and the stage schedule. One process note: this entry was approved/posted at partner level by Margaret Sullivan rather than routed to Daniel — it is already posted, so no further staging is needed.

Residual — parked per your instruction:
- The separate $4,390.62 variance on the WIP unbilled-services reconciliation (BL-75810CD0FEE4, account 119000) is left out of this cycle and parked as next-period cleanup, exactly as you asked. Hannah approved accept-timing for May; the underlying exception is left referenced as-is, to be revisited at FP-2026-06 BD3. The support memo and engagement-stage backup are filed and attached to the reconciliation.

One thing worth flagging before the package moves: the support narrative attributes the residual to a partial-success May feed retry, but the Brookfield Time & WIP feed actually ran clean for May (2,083/2,083 rows, no retry). That points the gap to the supporting/engagement-hours rollforward rather than a dropped GL feed batch. It does not change the May numbers or the accept-timing call, but we should test the rollforward side directly at the BD3 revisit. Happy to walk you through it.

Best,
George

content.text:
All done. Here's where the May Brookfield WIP recognition stands — and two things that change the read before this goes to Daniel.

## ⚠️ Read this first — two findings

**1. The $147,825 recognition entry is already POSTED — do NOT stage a second one.**
It exists as **JE-brookfield-brookfield_FP-2026-05-0057** (je_53962aed96fe4b67), status **posted**. You prepared it 5/31; it was approved *and* posted by **Margaret Sullivan** at partner level on 5/31 — it was **not** routed through Daniel. The figure ties out exactly:
- Audit & Assurance (401000) $89,400 + Tax (402000) $32,250 + Advisory (403000) $26,175 = **$147,825** ✓ (offset DR AR 110000 $89,425 + DR Deferred Rev 240000 $58,400).

So there was nothing to stage — staging another entry would have double-counted revenue. I did **not** create one. The recognition is recorded and reviewed at partner level, and Margaret has already green-lit finalizing the May MAP.

**2. The "partial feed batch" basis for the residual isn't supported by the feed record.**
Anaya's variance note (and the exception premise) say the May subledger feed retry produced a partial batch. But the **Brookfield Time & WIP feed ran clean for May** — run_e33ed2561f2c46, **2,083/2,083 rows, 0 rejected, no retry**. The only failure on that feed was FP-2026-03 (full failure, INC4471157); the "133000 partial" Jones cited in Slack was a different feed/account. Implication: the GL side of 119000 is complete, so the **$4,390.62 gap sits on the supporting/engagement-hours rollforward side, not a dropped GL feed**. Accept-timing still holds per Hannah's written approval, but the BD3 revisit should test the rollforward directly rather than wait for a feed re-run.

There's also an **open reviewer note from Edith Banda** you may not have seen on the recon (rn_564e65ce0d594f): *"FX revaluation entry referenced in the workbook — is this in scope?"* It's unresolved and will block recon approval/certification until cleared. I flagged it rather than guess the answer.
